translations.descricao: Screening for dyslipidemia is essential for the prevention of atherosclerotic cardiovascular events, focusing on the early identification of lipid alterations such as hypercholesterolemia and hypertriglyceridemia. In Brazil, dyslipidemia is a prevalent risk factor, contributing to high cardiovascular morbidity and mortality. The integrated approach considers the global cardiovascular risk.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening recommended for the prevention of cardiovascular events and pancreatitis in individuals with risk factors or ≥40 years old.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults ≥40 years; individuals <40 years with obesity, diabetes, hypertension, or family history of early cardiovascular disease.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The Brazilian Society of Cardiology (2017) and ESC guidelines recommend screening for cardiovascular risk assessment in adults.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: In Brazil, the prevalence of dyslipidemia is approximately 30-40% in adults, with hypercholesterolemia affecting 25% of the population. Globally, it is estimated at 39% for hypercholesterolemia.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence of dyslipidemia in adults is 5-10 cases per 1,000 inhabitants, associated with aging and obesity. In Brazil, it contributes to 20% of new cases of coronary disease.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Dyslipidemia contributes to 4.4 million global deaths from CVD annually. In Brazil, it represents a factor in 30% of cardiovascular deaths.
